title: Detect sub-second journal clock-order anomalies
prompt: The journalwindow helper already builds deterministic journalctl queries for unit, severity, boot, and time filters; preserves multiline messages; redacts configured literal tokens; and prints a shell-exact reproduction command. Its clock-order check, however, misses a backward realtime jump when both entries fall within the same wall-clock second. Fix the anomaly detection so it retains the journal's full microsecond precision. Continue to compare entries only within the same boot and only when monotonic time advances; equal or increasing realtime timestamps are not anomalies. Do not change the command-line interface, output format, redaction behavior, or tests.
